arts.ttl: Fiorentina vs. AC Milan to conclude matchday 7
arts.sum: Milan have won 78 of their 168 Serie A matches (D44 L46) against Fiorentina; only against Roma have the Rossoneri recorded more victories (80) in their top-flight history. Paulo Fonseca has won his last three Serie A matches as Milan manager; only once in the top-flight has the Portuguese coach recorded four successes in a row: between July and August 2020 with Roma.

arts.ttl: Serie A | Fiorentina v Milan: Match Preview
arts.sum: As Serie A action resumes this weekend, Fiorentina welcomes AC Milan to the Stadio Artemio Franchi for a highly anticipated Sunday night clash. Both teams enter the match with contrasting European results but share ambitions to climb the Serie A table. Fiorentina comes into this fixture after a hard-fought 2-0 victory over The New Saints in the UEFA Europa Conference League. However, their league form has been inconsistent under new manager Raffaele Palladino, with just one win in their opening six Serie A matches. arts.ttl: Pecchia: “Bologna are a very aggressive team, similar to Fiorentina in recent years”
arts.sum: On the eve of the Bologna-Parma match, Parma manager Fabio Pecchia shared his insights on the upcoming opponents during a press conference. Pecchia highlighted Bologna’s key characteristics, stating, “They are a very flexible team with many rotations, lots of players pushing forward, and very aggressive – similar to Fiorentina in recent years. They are a team capable of executing the offensive phase well.” When asked about strategies to contain Bologna’s midfield maestro Freuler, Pecchia emphasized the importance of focusing on the team as a whole rather than individuals. arts.ttl: Cardinale remains committed to Milan, aims to win “Intelligently” with healthy club and new stadium plans
arts.sum: Gerry Cardinale is holding firm on his long-term commitment to AC Milan, dispelling rumors of any potential sale or minority stake divestment. A RedBird spokesperson has refuted recent speculation about selling 22% of the club’s shares, reaffirming the goal of restoring Milan to its former glory. Calciomercato report that the strategy at RedBird is to “win intelligently,” avoiding reckless spending while continuing to increase revenues. This approach has already shown results, with the club reaching a record gross turnover of 457 million euros as of June 30th last year.
